sections.heading: SCOPE OF THE CODE
sections.title: Scope of the Code of Business Conduct
sections.summary: This section defines the scope of the Code of Business Conduct, stating it is part of the Fair Market Conduct Programme implemented by the Financial Services Commission (FSC). It outlines that the Code has 9 Guiding Principles which set general obligations for licensees to ensure sound business conduct. The objectives include promoting sound conduct in financial services, instilling confidence, preserving Mauritius' reputation as an international financial centre, and ensuring fair treatment of consumers and the public.

requirements.text: The Code of Business Conduct is part of the Fair Market Conduct Programme implemented by the Financial Services Commission
The Code has 9 Guiding Principles which set out general obligations for licensees to ensure sound business conduct

sections.source_quote: The Code of Business Conduct (the "Code") is part of the Fair Market Conduct Programme which the Financial Services Commission (the "Commission") is implementing.
sections.relevance_reason: Defines scope of business conduct for financial services

sections.heading: EFFECTIVE DATE
sections.title: Effective Date of the Code
sections.summary: This section specifies that the Code of Business Conduct comes into effect on 01 January 2016. It does not impose ongoing obligations but establishes a clear timeline for when the Code becomes operational for financial service licensees in Mauritius.

requirements.text: The Code shall come into effect on 01 January 2016

sections.source_quote: The Code shall come into effect on 01 January 2016.

sections.heading: explanatory notes have been included.
sections.title: Nine Guiding Principles for Financial Service Licensees
sections.summary: This section details nine Guiding Principles that licensees must follow to ensure sound business conduct. Each principle includes specific obligations such as acting with due skill and care, maintaining business integrity, avoiding conflicts of interest, communicating with customers, protecting customer assets, ensuring compliance with regulations, maintaining adequate financial resources, managing internal affairs responsibly, and cooperating with the Financial Services Commission.

requirements.text: Licensees must act with due skill, care and diligence towards their customers and other stakeholders
Licensees must observe high standards of honesty, integrity and fairness and ensure all business transactions are carried out and recorded fairly and accurately
Licensees must avoid situations of conflict of interests and ensure fair treatment of all customers if a conflict arises
Licensees must seek necessary information about customers' individual circumstances and financial objectives
Licensees must provide customers with accurate, timely and comprehensible information to enable informed decisions
Licensees must ensure client assets are properly segregated and identifiable at all times
Licensees must observe high standards of market conduct and comply with all regulatory requirements applicable to their business activities
Licensees must maintain adequate financial resources to meet financial obligations and implement appropriate risk management policies
Licensees must manage business in a responsible and sustainable manner while ensuring adequate controls and proper systems and procedures
Licensees must deal with the Commission in a cooperative manner and comply with disclosure obligations promptly

sections.source_quote: The Guiding Principles are intended to ensure that licensees conduct their business in a way that promotes the best interests of consumers and the integrity of the financial services industry.
sections.relevance_reason: Defines data handling duties for financial services

sections.heading: COMPLIANCE WITH THE CODE
sections.title: Compliance with the Code of Business Conduct
sections.summary: This section mandates that all financial service licensees must comply with the Code of Business Conduct. It specifies that the Board and other officers are responsible for setting the tone and guiding business practices to ensure employees and third parties contribute to sound business conduct. Licensees must also maintain records of compliance arrangements for inspection by the Financial Services Commission.

requirements.text: All licensees must comply with the Code
The Board and other officers of licensees are responsible for setting the tone and guiding business practices to ensure employees and third parties contribute to sound business conduct
Licensees must keep appropriate records of arrangements made to comply with the Code for inspection by the Commission upon request
Non-compliance with the Code will expose the licensee to regulatory actions in accordance with relevant Acts

sections.source_quote: All licensees must comply with the Code.
